reason: The remediation only edits text files (/etc/profile and scripts in /etc/profile.d) to set a standard umask value. This change does not affect authentication, network services, or boot configuration, and it will not lock out any user. It follows standard STIG guidance and can be applied safely with a simple configuration edit.
estimated_impact: filesystems affected; Potential for overly permissive default file permissions if umask is set incorrectly; Incorrect syntax could cause profile parsing errors
